notes: PRAGMA table_info is used for runtime column detection (canonical_file_path, document_type). Turso/libSQL supports PRAGMA but column introspection semantics may differ.
Full DDL with AUTOINCREMENT, strftime default, CHECK constraint, and JSON text columns. Turso supports all but verify AUTOINCREMENT semantics.
Expression index using json_extract on JSON array element. Turso/libSQL lacks json_extract in index expressions — this index cannot be created as-is.
BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE triggers enforce append-only immutability. Turso/libSQL lacks CREATE TRIGGER — this enforcement must move to application code.
DELETE trigger for append-only enforcement. Turso/libSQL lacks triggers — must be replaced with application-level guard.
json_each() table-valued function used to expand JSON arrays for JOIN queries. Turso/libSQL lacks json_each — must rewrite as application-side JSON parsing.
json_extract in WHERE clause for query filtering. Turso supports json_extract in queries but not in indexes.
Queries sqlite_master for trigger verification. Turso/libSQL has sqlite_master but triggers don't exist — verifyAppendOnly will always return false.
Three db.exec calls use raw SQL DDL including CREATE TRIGGER with BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE and RAISE(ABORT). Turso lacks CREATE TRIGGER support; append-only enforcement must move to application layer.
Index expression uses json_extract and CAST inside CREATE INDEX. Turso does not support json_extract in index expressions; must denormalize or drop this index.
BEFORE UPDATE and BEFORE DELETE triggers with RAISE(ABORT) enforce append-only. Turso has no trigger support; immutability must be enforced in application code.
Uses json_each() table-valued function to expand JSON arrays in JOIN. Turso does not support json_each; must denormalize linked_memory_ids into a junction table.
WHERE clause uses json_extract on decision_meta column. Turso lacks json_extract; must extract normalizedPath into a dedicated column.
Queries sqlite_master for trigger existence. Turso sqlite_master may not list triggers if unsupported; verification logic needs rework.
PRAGMA table_info for schema introspection. Turso/libSQL may return different PRAGMA result shapes; verify compatibility.
Turso/libSQL lacks .pragma() method and PRAGMA result-set semantics differ; schema introspection must use libSQL-specific API or INFORMATION_SCHEMA
better-sqlite3 synchronous transaction wrapper; libSQL client is async-first and transaction API differs fundamentally
better-sqlite3 .run() returns { changes, lastInsertRowid }; libSQL async execute() returns different result shape requiring adapter
better-sqlite3 .exec() runs multi-statement DDL synchronously; libSQL .execute() is async and multi-statement batching differs
Trigger DDL syntax with BEGIN/END blocks; Turso/libSQL trigger support may differ in RAISE() semantics and BEFORE triggers
JSON function in index expression is SQLite 3.38+; Turso/libSQL JSON support may not cover expression indexes on json_extract
Table-valued function json_each() for array expansion; supported in libSQL but semantics may diverge for nested JSON
better-sqlite3 result property lastInsertRowid; libSQL async execute returns different result structure needing adapter
better-sqlite3 synchronous .prepare().all(); libSQL async execute() requires await and different parameter passing convention
better-sqlite3 type namespace used throughout; migration requires replacing with libSQL client types and async wrappers
